title: Spike-driven Transformer
abstract: The paper proposes a novel Spike-driven Transformer that achieves the spike-driven nature of Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) throughout the network while maintaining good task performance. It redesigns two core modules of Transformer (Vanilla Self-Attention and Multi-Layer Perceptron) to have a spike-driven paradigm.
key_contributions: Redesign of Vanilla Self-Attention (VSA) to Spike-Driven Self-Attention (SDSA)
Redesign of Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) to have a spike-driven paradigm
Achieving spike-driven nature throughout the network

technical_details.approach: Uses only binary spikes for communication between neurons
technical_details.time_complexity: Linear with both token and channel dimensions
technical_details.energy_consumption: Almost no energy consumption for the self-attention operator

problem_addressed: Previous spiking Transformers had poor task accuracy
Existing approaches relied on hybrid computing, which limited the benefits of SNN's low energy cost
Current spiking Transformers are hardly usable on neuromorphic chips

methodology.focus: Redesigning core Transformer modules (VSA and MLP) to have a spike-driven paradigm
methodology.operations: Only mask and sparse addition operations

results: Achieves spike-driven nature throughout the network
Has linear time complexity with both token and channel dimensions
Has almost no energy consumption for the self-attention operator
